Name: UI-LOGIN-001 - Successful login with valid credentials and 2FA
Objective: Verify that successful login with valid credentials and 2FA.
Precondition: Merchant account exists with email/password and 2FA (authenticator app) enrolled.
Step: 1. Enter valid email and password on the sign-in screen.
Expected Result: Fields accept input and the 2FA challenge screen is presented.

Step: 2. Enter the correct 6-digit 2FA code.
Expected Result: Code is accepted.

Step: 3. Click 'Sign In'.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and lands on the Dashboard; a session token is issued.

Step: 4. Attempt to reuse the same 2FA code on a second login.
Expected Result: The 2FA code cannot be reused (single-use).

Name: UI-LOGIN-002 - Login is rejected with an incorrect 2FA code
Objective: Verify that login is rejected with an incorrect 2FA code.
Precondition: Merchant has entered correct email/password.
Step: 1. Enter a valid password.
Expected Result: Password is accepted and the 2FA challenge is presented.

Step: 2. Enter an incorrect 6-digit code.
Expected Result: Code is rejected.

Step: 3. Click 'Verify'.
Expected Result: Login is rejected with inline error 'Invalid verification code'; user remains on the 2FA challenge screen; attempt is counted toward lockout threshold.

Name: UI-LOGIN-003 - Account locks out after repeated failed login attempts
Objective: Verify that account locks out after repeated failed login attempts.
Precondition: Merchant account is in a normal, unlocked state.
Step: 1. Attempt login with an incorrect password 5 times in a row.
Expected Result: Each attempt is rejected; failed-attempt counter increments.

Step: 2. Attempt a 6th login with the correct password.
Expected Result: Account is temporarily locked after the configured threshold; the 6th (correct) attempt is also rejected with a 'temporarily locked, try again later' message.

Name: UI-DASH-001 - Transaction list displays recent transactions on load
Objective: Verify that transaction list displays recent transactions on load.
Precondition: Merchant has at least 10 transactions in the last 30 days.
Step: 1. Log in.
Expected Result: User reaches the Dashboard.

Step: 2. Navigate to the Transactions tab.
Expected Result: List loads showing transaction ID, date, amount, currency, status, and masked card; sorted newest-first by default.

Name: UI-DASH-002 - Filtering transactions by date range
Objective: Verify that filtering transactions by date range.
Precondition: Merchant has transactions spanning several months.
Step: 1. Open the date-range filter.
Expected Result: Date-range picker is displayed.

Step: 2. Select a specific start and end date, then apply the filter.
Expected Result: Only transactions with a created date inside the selected range are shown; result count updates; filter chip is visible and removable.

Name: UI-DASH-003 - Filtering transactions by status
Objective: Verify that filtering transactions by status.
Precondition: Merchant has transactions in AUTHORIZED, CAPTURED, REFUNDED, and DECLINED states.
Step: 1. Open the status filter.
Expected Result: Status options are listed.

Step: 2. Select 'Refunded' only.
Expected Result: Only REFUNDED transactions are displayed; other statuses are excluded.

Step: 3. Clear the filter.
Expected Result: Full list is restored.

Name: UI-DASH-004 - Filtering transactions by currency
Objective: Verify that filtering transactions by currency.
Precondition: Merchant processes transactions in multiple currencies (AED, USD, EUR).
Step: 1. Open the currency filter and select 'USD'.
Expected Result: Only USD-denominated transactions are shown; amounts display with the correct currency symbol/code.

Name: UI-DASH-005 - Combining multiple filters narrows results correctly
Objective: Verify that combining multiple filters narrows results correctly.
Precondition: Merchant has a mixed transaction history.
Step: 1. Apply a date range filter.
Expected Result: Result set narrows to the date range.

Step: 2. Additionally apply a status filter.
Expected Result: Result set narrows further to CAPTURED transactions within the range.

Step: 3. Additionally apply a currency filter.
Expected Result: Filters are applied cumulatively (AND logic); result set matches all three conditions simultaneously.

Step: 4. Clear one of the three filters.
Expected Result: Results re-widen correctly to reflect the remaining active filters.

Name: UI-DET-001 - Transaction detail view shows full lifecycle history
Objective: Verify that transaction detail view shows full lifecycle history.
Precondition: A transaction has been authorized, captured, and partially refunded.
Step: 1. Click a transaction row to open its detail view.
Expected Result: Detail page shows a timeline of state changes with timestamps (Authorized -> Captured -> Partially Refunded), original amount, and remaining refundable balance.

Name: UI-DET-002 - Initiating a full refund from the transaction detail view
Objective: Verify that initiating a full refund from the transaction detail view.
Precondition: Transaction is in CAPTURED state with no prior refunds.
Step: 1. Open the transaction detail view.
Expected Result: Detail view is displayed.

Step: 2. Click 'Refund', select 'Full amount', and confirm.
Expected Result: Refund is submitted; status updates to REFUNDED.

Step: 3. Observe the confirmation and updated timeline.
Expected Result: A confirmation toast/message is shown; refund appears in the transaction timeline.

Name: UI-DET-003 - Initiating a partial refund with amount validation
Objective: Verify that initiating a partial refund with amount validation.
Precondition: Transaction is CAPTURED, amount AED 250.00.
Step: 1. Click 'Refund' and enter a partial amount.
Expected Result: Field accepts the entered amount.

Step: 2. Confirm the refund.
Expected Result: Refund succeeds; status updates to PARTIALLY_REFUNDED; remaining refundable balance shown as AED 150.00.

Name: UI-DET-004 - Refund amount exceeding the refundable balance is blocked in the UI
Objective: Verify that refund amount exceeding the refundable balance is blocked in the UI.
Precondition: Transaction is CAPTURED, AED 250.00, with AED 150.00 already refunded.
Step: 1. Click 'Refund'.
Expected Result: Refund form is displayed.

Step: 2. Enter an amount greater than the remaining AED 100.00 balance.
Expected Result: 'Refund' button is disabled or an inline validation error appears ('Amount exceeds refundable balance') before submission; no request is sent.

Name: UI-DET-005 - Double-clicking 'Confirm Refund' does not create two refunds
Objective: Verify that double-clicking 'Confirm Refund' does not create two refunds.
Precondition: Transaction is eligible for a full refund.
Step: 1. Click 'Refund', enter amount, click 'Confirm'.
Expected Result: Refund submission begins.

Step: 2. Immediately click 'Confirm' again before the page responds.
Expected Result: Button is disabled after first click / a loading state is shown; only one refund request is submitted and processed.

Name: UI-SET-001 - Viewing the settlement/payout report for a completed period
Objective: Verify that viewing the settlement/payout report for a completed period.
Precondition: Merchant has at least one completed settlement period.
Step: 1. Navigate to the 'Settlements' tab.
Expected Result: Settlement periods are listed.

Step: 2. Select a past period.
Expected Result: Report shows gross transaction volume, fees deducted, refunds, and net payout amount; totals reconcile (gross - fees - refunds = net payout).

Name: UI-SET-002 - Exporting a settlement report as CSV
Objective: Verify that exporting a settlement report as CSV.
Precondition: A settlement report is displayed on screen.
Step: 1. Click 'Export CSV'.
Expected Result: CSV downloads successfully.

Step: 2. Open the downloaded file.
Expected Result: Column headers and row totals match the on-screen report exactly.

Name: UI-KEY-001 - Generating a new API key
Objective: Verify that generating a new API key.
Precondition: Merchant is logged in with admin privileges and navigates to the API Keys screen.
Step: 1. Click 'Generate New Key' and name the key.
Expected Result: Key generation form accepts the label.

Step: 2. Confirm.
Expected Result: New key is generated and shown in full exactly once; a warning states it will not be shown again.

Step: 3. Reload the API Keys list.
Expected Result: Key appears in the list afterward only in masked form.

Name: UI-KEY-002 - Revoking an existing API key
Objective: Verify that revoking an existing API key.
Precondition: At least one active API key exists.
Step: 1. Click 'Revoke' next to a key and confirm in the dialog.
Expected Result: Key status changes to 'Revoked'.

Step: 2. Attempt an API call using the revoked key.
Expected Result: The API call fails with an authentication error (cross-checked against API-ERR-001).

Name: UI-KEY-003 - Revoking an API key requires explicit confirmation
Objective: Verify that revoking an API key requires explicit confirmation.
Precondition: An active API key exists.
Step: 1. Click 'Revoke' and observe the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: Dialog clearly warns that revocation is immediate and irreversible.

Step: 2. Click 'Cancel'.
Expected Result: Key is NOT revoked after cancelling.

Name: UI-DISP-001 - Amounts display with correct currency symbol and decimal formatting
Objective: Verify that amounts display with correct currency symbol and decimal formatting.
Precondition: Merchant has transactions in AED, USD, and a zero-decimal currency.
Step: 1. View the transaction list containing mixed currencies.
Expected Result: Each amount shows the correct symbol/code and decimal precision for its currency (e.g., zero-decimal currencies show no decimal places); thousands separators render correctly.

Name: UI-DISP-002 - Portal renders correctly across supported browsers and a tablet viewport
Objective: Verify that portal renders correctly across supported browsers and a tablet viewport.
Precondition: Test environment has Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and a tablet-width (768px) viewport available.
Step: 1. Load the Dashboard in each browser/viewport.
Expected Result: Dashboard renders in each target environment.

Step: 2. Check layout, navigation, and table readability.
Expected Result: No broken layout, overlapping elements, or horizontal scroll issues; core navigation and transaction table remain usable at tablet width.

Name: UI-PERM-001 - Support-role user sees masked card data only, not full PAN
Objective: Verify that support-role user sees masked card data only, not full PAN.
Precondition: A user account is assigned the 'Support' role (read-only, limited PII visibility).
Step: 1. Log in as a Support-role user.
Expected Result: User reaches the Dashboard with Support-role permissions.

Step: 2. Open a transaction detail view.
Expected Result: Only masked card (last 4 digits) and brand are visible; no UI control or hidden field exposes the full PAN.

Step: 3. Inspect the underlying API response backing the view.
Expected Result: API responses backing the view are also masked.

Name: UI-PERM-002 - Support-role user cannot access API Key Management or issue refunds
Objective: Verify that support-role user cannot access API Key Management or issue refunds.
Precondition: Support-role user is logged in.
Step: 1. Attempt to navigate directly to the API Keys URL.
Expected Result: Navigation is blocked with a 'not authorized' page or the menu item is absent.

Step: 2. Attempt to open a transaction and locate a 'Refund' control.
Expected Result: 'Refund' action is not present/is disabled for this role.

Name: UI-WAL-001 - Wallet balance displays correctly on the Wallet home screen
Objective: Verify that wallet balance displays correctly on the Wallet home screen.
Precondition: Consumer is logged in and has a non-zero wallet balance.
Step: 1. Log in to the NexaPay Wallet app.
Expected Result: Login succeeds and the Wallet home screen loads.

Step: 2. Observe the balance display on the Wallet home screen.
Expected Result: Current available balance is displayed prominently, correctly formatted with currency symbol and two-decimal precision; balance matches the backend ledger total.

Name: UI-WAL-002 - Wallet top-up flow: selecting a funding source and entering an amount
Objective: Verify that wallet top-up flow: selecting a funding source and entering an amount.
Precondition: Consumer has at least one linked bank card and a bank-transfer option available.
Step: 1. Tap 'Top Up' and select a funding source.
Expected Result: Funding source selection is accepted.

Step: 2. Enter an amount and confirm.
Expected Result: Confirmation screen appears before submission; a valid amount proceeds to submission.

Step: 3. Enter an amount below the configured minimum.
Expected Result: Amount is blocked with inline validation.

Name: UI-WAL-003 - Wallet top-up confirmation screen shows the updated balance
Objective: Verify that wallet top-up confirmation screen shows the updated balance.
Precondition: A top-up of AED 200.00 has just completed successfully.
Step: 1. Complete a top-up and observe the success/confirmation screen.
Expected Result: Success screen shows the amount credited and a transaction reference.

Step: 2. Return to the Wallet home screen.
Expected Result: Wallet home screen balance reflects the new total without requiring a manual refresh.

Name: UI-P2P-001 - P2P transfer: recipient lookup by phone number or username
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer: recipient lookup by phone number or username.
Precondition: Consumer wants to send money to another NexaPay user.
Step: 1. Tap 'Send Money' and enter a recipient's phone number or username.
Expected Result: Lookup is performed.

Step: 2. Observe the lookup result for a valid recipient.
Expected Result: Matching recipient's masked name/profile is shown for confirmation before any amount is entered.

Step: 3. Enter a phone number/username with no matching NexaPay user.
Expected Result: A clear 'no NexaPay user found' state is shown.

Name: UI-P2P-002 - P2P transfer confirmation screen before submission
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer confirmation screen before submission.
Precondition: A valid recipient has been found and an amount entered.
Step: 1. Enter a transfer amount and tap 'Continue'.
Expected Result: Confirmation screen is displayed.

Step: 2. Review the confirmation screen before the final 'Send' action.
Expected Result: Confirmation screen clearly shows recipient name, amount, and any fee (if applicable); amount and recipient cannot be silently altered after this screen.

Name: UI-P2P-003 - P2P transfer receipt/success screen with transaction reference
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer receipt/success screen with transaction reference.
Precondition: A P2P transfer has just completed successfully.
Step: 1. Confirm and send a transfer.
Expected Result: Transfer completes successfully.

Step: 2. Observe the success/receipt screen.
Expected Result: Receipt screen shows amount, recipient, timestamp, and a unique transaction reference.

Step: 3. Navigate to transaction history and locate the receipt.
Expected Result: Receipt is also retrievable later from transaction history.

Name: UI-P2P-004 - P2P transfer is blocked in the UI when the amount exceeds available balance
Objective: Verify that p2P transfer is blocked in the UI when the amount exceeds available balance.
Precondition: Wallet balance is AED 30.00.
Step: 1. Tap 'Send Money' and select a valid recipient.
Expected Result: Recipient is selected.

Step: 2. Enter an amount greater than the available balance.
Expected Result: 'Continue'/'Send' action is disabled or an inline validation error appears ('Insufficient balance') before submission; no transfer request is sent.

Name: UI-BILL-001 - Bill payment: biller selection from a category list
Objective: Verify that bill payment: biller selection from a category list.
Precondition: Consumer wants to pay a utility or telecom bill.
Step: 1. Tap 'Pay Bills' and browse or search biller categories.
Expected Result: Biller categories and billers load correctly.

Step: 2. Select a specific biller.
Expected Result: Selecting a biller proceeds to the reference-entry screen for that biller.

Name: UI-BILL-002 - Bill payment: biller reference entry validation
Objective: Verify that bill payment: biller reference entry validation.
Precondition: A biller has been selected that requires a fixed-format account/reference number.
Step: 1. Enter a biller reference in an invalid format.
Expected Result: Field accepts the input for validation.

Step: 2. Attempt to continue.
Expected Result: Inline validation error is shown before submission ('Enter a valid account/reference number'); 'Continue' remains disabled until a validly formatted reference is entered.

Name: UI-BILL-003 - Bill payment confirmation and success screen
Objective: Verify that bill payment confirmation and success screen.
Precondition: A valid biller reference and amount have been entered.
Step: 1. Review the confirmation screen showing biller, reference, and amount.
Expected Result: Confirmation screen requires explicit confirmation before charging the wallet.

Step: 2. Confirm payment and observe the success screen.
Expected Result: Success screen shows a Pending/Success status consistent with the backend, with a transaction reference.

Name: UI-HIST-001 - Transaction history filtering by wallet activity type
Objective: Verify that transaction history filtering by wallet activity type.
Precondition: Wallet history contains a mix of top-ups, P2P transfers, and bill payments.
Step: 1. Navigate to 'Transaction History' and apply a filter for 'P2P Transfers' only.
Expected Result: List narrows to only P2P transfer entries showing recipient details.

Step: 2. Switch the filter to 'Bill Payments' only.
Expected Result: List narrows to only bill payment entries showing biller details.

Step: 3. Clear all filters.
Expected Result: Full combined history is restored.
